analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: float32 (inferred: nn.Linear default + torch.randn default; no autocast in workload)

operators: aten::mm op_id=3/5/7/9/13/15/17/19 (8 nodes, 1 GEMM kernel each)

bottleneck.description: Every matmul (the opaque `Kernel2` GEMM) executes on the SIMT FP32 CUDA-core path with Tensor Cores fully idle. tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 and smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active = 0 on ALL GEMM kernels (k_00015..k_00053). The model is FP32 (nn.Linear default weights + torch.randn FP32 input, no autocast), and on Blackwell an FP32 mm does not route to the tensor-core path. The result is a register-pressure-limited SIMT GEMM: 200-210 registers/thread caps achieved_occupancy at 8.3-16.6% and sm_throughput at 6-23%, while DRAM throughput is only 2.8-9.1% — the kernel is neither memory-bound nor compute-saturated; it is wasting the chip's primary matmul datapath.

transformation.description: Insert dtype casts so each matmul runs on the Blackwell tensor-core datapath instead of the FP32 SIMT path. At the Aten IR level, wrap every `torch.ops.aten.mm.default` node: cast both operands to bf16 with `aten.to`, run the mm in bf16 (Inductor selects the bf16 TC GEMM template), then cast the result back to fp32 before the bias-add/activation epilogue. This engages the currently-idle tensor cores. bf16 keeps the 8-bit exponent of fp32 (no overflow risk for this MLP) and the per-layer requantization is negligible against the GEMM cost. A lower-risk variant that requires no graph surgery is enabling TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True), which reroutes the FP32 mm to TF32 tensor cores; bf16 is faster on Blackwell and is the recommended graph-level form.
transformation.location: Custom torch.compile backend FX pass, run on the post-AOTAutograd Aten graph before Inductor lowering.
fx_steps: if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default:
back.update_arg(0, node)  # restore self-reference broken by replace_all_uses_with
transformation.code_hint: Register via torch._dynamo.register_backend; in the backend, run this pass then delegate to torch._inductor.compile_fx.compile_fx(gm, example_inputs). Keep the final-layer output cast so downstream consumers still see fp32.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: bf16 halves weight/activation GEMM traffic; primary win is compute datapath, not bandwidth (GEMMs are SIMT-bound, not DRAM-bound at 3-9% DRAM).

optimizations.notes: Confidence is HIGH on the mechanism (tensor_core_active_pct is exactly 0 across all 19 GEMM kernels on a Blackwell part — the matmul datapath is unused). Confidence is MEDIUM on the exact 59% magnitude: duration_ns is ncu-inflated 2-5x so used only for relative comparison; the ~3-6x typical bf16-TC speedup for these [256x2048]/[2048x2048]/[2048x512] shapes is throttled by the small M=256 dimension limiting wave occupancy, so a conservative 60% per-GEMM reduction is used. Validate numerics with an allclose check (bf16 atol~1e-2).

bottleneck.description: Each layer launches a separate small pointwise kernel (triton_poi_fused_addmm_relu_0, _gelu_1, _silu_2, _tanh_3) immediately after its GEMM to do bias-add + activation. On the FP32 SIMT path Inductor cannot fuse this epilogue into the GEMM (the SIMT Kernel2 has no epilogue hook), so it pays a separate kernel launch + a full read of the [256,2048] (or [256,512]) GEMM output from L2/DRAM and a write-back per layer. Individually each is tiny (1.7-2.4k ns, dram_throughput up to ~35%, sm_throughput 1-11%) but it is pure launch + round-trip overhead that the tensor-core matmul template can absorb.

transformation.description: After OPT-1 puts the GEMMs on the Inductor bf16 matmul template, enable epilogue fusion so the bias-add and the per-layer activation (relu/gelu/silu/tanh) are fused INTO the GEMM template, eliminating the separate pointwise kernels and the GEMM-output round-trip. At the Aten IR level the relevant epilogue nodes are aten.add.Tensor (bias), aten.relu.default, aten.gelu.default, aten.silu.default, aten.tanh.default sitting on the output of each aten.mm.default. The pass marks these as fusible epilogues of the preceding mm so Inductor's max-autotune matmul template (config.max_autotune_gemm / epilogue_fusion=True) generates a single fused kernel per layer.
transformation.location: Same custom backend; set torch._inductor.config.epilogue_fusion = True and torch._inductor.config.max_autotune_gemm = True before compile_fx, OR an FX pass tagging mm->pointwise chains as fusion groups. Must run after OPT-1.
fx_steps: # Enable in backend before compile_fx (config-level, no node surgery required):
ind_cfg.max_autotune_gemm = True   # selects Triton TC matmul template with epilogue support
# Optional explicit FX tagging for the mm -> (add bias) -> activation chain:
if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target is torch.ops.aten.mm.default:
if user.target is torch.ops.aten.add.Tensor or user.target in ACTS:
user.meta['inductor_epilogue_of'] = node  # consumed by template selection
transformation.code_hint: The activation kernels currently re-read the full GEMM output tile; fusing into the matmul template removes 4 kernel launches per iteration and the L2/DRAM round-trip of the [256,2048] hidden activations.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes one full read+write of each layer's GEMM output (3x [256,2048] + 1x [256,512] per iteration) from the activation kernels by fusing into the matmul epilogue.

optimizations.notes: Small absolute win (epilogues are 0.7% of attributed time) but it is essentially free once OPT-1 is in place and removes 4 launches + activation round-trips per iteration. MUST come after OPT-1: epilogue fusion into the GEMM requires the bf16 Triton matmul template; the FP32 SIMT Kernel2 GEMM has no epilogue hook, so running this before OPT-1 finds nothing fusible. Confidence capped at medium (no fx-patterns.md / web corroboration available this session).

global_notes: 1 operator below 1% threshold omitted (aten::addmm_0 at 0.70% of attributed time); its kernels are also analyzed in OPT-2 because they are the per-layer activation epilogues.

fused_kernel_double_count flag is ACTIVE and significant: the same physical GEMM kernels are attributed three times — once in the NVTX roll-up aten::mm_0 (is_fused implied, 7 kernels), once in layer::unique::prologue (is_fused=true, 17 kernels incl. GEMMs+activations), and once per discrete aten::mm op_id=N entry (1 kernel each, the clean torch_profiler/NVTX 1:1 attributions). The percentages in time_budget therefore sum across overlapping views; the TRUE per-iteration GEMM workload is the 8 discrete op_id GEMMs (~875k ns of Kernel2 across the run). Treat OPT-1's reduction_pct relative to the real (de-duplicated) GEMM share, which is ~99% of unique compute.

dynamic_shapes flag is a MECHANICAL trigger only: 'Kernel2' is an opaque, reused name covering three distinct GEMM shapes ([512->2048], [2048->2048], [2048->512]), so same-name duration CV exceeds 50%. This is shape heterogeneity under a shared kernel name, NOT runtime dynamic shapes — all shapes are static (BATCH_SIZE=256 fixed).

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null on every operator (expected: removed on Blackwell). Latency-bound assessment used eligible_cycles_pct instead: GEMM ops sit at 12-36% eligible cycles with 8-17% occupancy, consistent with the register-pressure-limited FP32 SIMT GEMM diagnosed in OPT-1.

Knowledge files knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were not present in the repo this session, and context7/exa-search MCP tools were unavailable. Hardware context (Blackwell: no tensor-core engagement on FP32 mm, warp_cycles_per_instruction removed) was applied from model knowledge; novel/uncorroborated transforms (OPT-2 epilogue fusion) are capped at medium confidence per fallback policy. OPT-1's mechanism does not depend on the missing files — it is read directly from tensor_core_active_pct=0 on a Blackwell GPU.

No multi-stream overlap (all kernels stream_id=7), no CUDA-graph replay (inductor mode), no unattributed kernels, timestamps in normal range. The 15 pre-NVTX initialization kernels in warnings[] were correctly excluded from attribution.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (dtype_promotion) -> OPT-2 (epilogue fusion). No register_buffer-bearing passes (no QKV/SDPA in this MLP), so the standard 'dtype promotion before buffer allocation' constraint does not bind here. Apply order: OPT-1 then OPT-2.
